script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah.
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Morning, Alex.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It's July 21st, 2026. Another Tuesday, right?
script.ja: 2026年7月21日、また火曜日ですね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It is.
script.ja: そうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: And welcome to The Daily Tanu-chan Show! Hope you're all ready to tackle Tuesday.
script.ja: そして、ザ・デイリー・タヌちゃんショーへようこそ！みんな、火曜日を乗り切る準備はできていますか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Hope everyone's ready for the day.
script.ja: 皆さんが今日一日を頑張れるといいわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Wow, it's already heating up, isn't it? The forecast says mainly clear, high 35.6 today.
script.ja: うわー、もう暑くなってきましたね。今日の予報は、ほとんど晴れで最高気温35.6度だそうです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yeah, it's going to be a hot one.
script.ja: ええ、暑い一日になりそうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: And 88% chance of rain? How does that work? Clear and rain? I never get that.
script.ja: そして降水確率88%？どういうことですか？晴れなのに雨？いつも理解できません。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Just means a high chance of a sudden downpour later, probably.
script.ja: たぶん、後で突然の土砂降りの可能性が高いってことよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right. Still, need my coffee. It's non-negotiable.
script.ja: ですね。とにかくコーヒーが必要です。これだけは譲れません。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: And I've got my tea ready.
script.ja: 私はお茶を用意したわ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Did you see the news about that missing boy in Kagoshima?
script.ja: 鹿児島で行方不明になっていた男の子のニュース、見た？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, the five-year-old? I saw a headline. They found a body, didn't they?
script.ja: ああ、5歳の子ですか？見出しを見ました。遺体が見つかったんですよね？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They believe it's him. In a river near the hot spring.
script.ja: 彼だと考えられているわ。温泉の近くの川で。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ah, that's just... awful. My heart goes out to his family. You just never know, do you?
script.ja: ああ、それはただ…ひどいですね。ご家族にお悔やみ申し上げます。本当に何が起こるかわからないものですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: No, you don't.
script.ja: ええ、そうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Well, on a completely different note, I saw something about foreign visitors to Japan dipping.
script.ja: さて、全く別の話ですが、訪日外国人が減少しているというニュースを見ました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, down 2 percent in the first half of the year. Chinese travelers specifically.
script.ja: ええ、上半期で2%減少ね。特に中国人観光客が。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Hmm. I wonder what that means for... well, everything. Our sales, maybe?
script.ja: うーん。それが...まあ、全てにどう影響するんでしょうね。もしかしたら僕たちの売上にも？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It could affect various sectors.
script.ja: さまざまな分野に影響を与える可能性はあるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, I guess so. Though at my office, I feel like we always hear about dips and surges, but nothing ever really changes.
script.ja: ええ、そうでしょうね。でも、僕のオフィスでは、いつも増減の話を聞くけど、結局何も変わらないような気がします。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Things tend to balance out in the long term.
script.ja: 物事は長期的には均衡する傾向にあるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Okay, this one was interesting. A Tokyo music venue looking for a band to live on the premises and perform every day for a week.
script.ja: さて、これは面白かったですね。東京のあるライブハウスが、一週間敷地内に住み込みで毎日演奏するバンドを探しているそうです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That sounds like an intense week.
script.ja: それはかなり大変な一週間になりそうね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right? Like a musical boot camp. I bet some young bands would jump at that, though. Imagine the practice you'd get.
script.ja: でしょう？音楽のブートキャンプみたい。でも、若いバンドなら飛びつくでしょうね。どれだけ練習できるか想像してみてください。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's a unique opportunity, definitely.
script.ja: 間違いなく、ユニークな機会ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, speaking of unique, I was looking up 'Today's Theme' for the show, and I totally mixed up the date.
script.ja: あ、ユニークといえば、番組の「今日のテーマ」を調べていたんですが、完全に日付を間違えていました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: (A small, quiet chuckle) What did you find?
script.ja: （小さく静かに笑い）何を見つけたの？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It's actually for tomorrow, July 22nd. It's 'Geta Day'.
script.ja: 実は明日、7月22日のことでした。「下駄の日」です。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Ah, 下駄の日. Yes, I know that one.
script.ja: ああ、下駄の日ね。それは知っているわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: So, geta, the traditional Japanese wooden clogs. Why the 22nd?
script.ja: 下駄、日本の伝統的な木製の履物ですね。なぜ22日なんですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's because of the numbers. Geta often used a measurement of 'seven sun, seven bu', which is 7寸7分. So, '7' for July. And the two parts of the geta, the 'teeth', look like the character for 'two', 二. So, July 22nd.
script.ja: 数字が理由よ。下駄はよく「七寸七分」という寸法が使われたから「7月」。そして下駄の歯の部分が「二」の字に見えるから、22日なの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, I didn't know that! That's clever. I usually just think of them for summer festivals.
script.ja: へえ、知りませんでした！それは賢いですね。僕はいつも夏祭りの時くらいしか考えません。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: You know, with this heat, all I can think about is a good sento or a sauna. I went last weekend, after putting the baby to sleep.
script.ja: この暑さだと、良い銭湯かサウナのことばかり考えちゃいますね。先週末、赤ちゃんを寝かしつけた後に行きました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: That must have been nice. A little escape.
script.ja: それは良かったわね。ちょっとした現実逃避。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: It was. That feeling of 'totonou'... just completely relaxed. My mind goes totally blank. It's an *ephemeral* moment of peace, but I really crave it these days.
script.ja: ええ、そうでした。「ととのう」って感覚…完全にリラックスして、頭が真っ白になるんです。つかの間の平和な瞬間なんですが、最近本当にそれを求めているんです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Ephemeral?
script.ja: エフェメラル？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Yeah, like, temporary? Fleeting? Just for a short time. My little one is growing up so fast, and everything changes every day. It makes me realize how quickly these moments pass.
script.ja: はい、一時的とか、はかない、とか。短い時間のことです。うちの子は本当にあっという間に大きくなって、毎日色々なことが変わるんです。こういう瞬間がいかに早く過ぎ去るかを実感しますね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: They do grow quickly at that age. Mine are teenagers now. Still at home, but very different challenges.
script.ja: あの頃はあっという間に大きくなるわね。うちの子たちはもうティーンエイジャーよ。まだ家にいるけど、また全然違う大変さがあるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I can imagine. My baby's just over a year old now. It's the best, but man, the sleep deprivation is real. Sometimes I feel like I'm barely holding on.
script.ja: 想像できます。うちの子は今1歳ちょっとです。最高なんですが、もう、睡眠不足が本当に辛くて。時々、ギリギリで持ちこたえているような気がします。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It gets better eventually.
script.ja: いずれは良くなるわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I hope so. This summer heat is not helping. It feels like the *culmination* of all the stress this year.
script.ja: だといいんですが。この夏の暑さも全然助けになってないし。今年のストレスの全てが凝縮されているような気がします。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Culmination?
script.ja: カルミネーション？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Uh, like the highest point, or the end result of something. The peak. This summer is definitely peaking in heat and stress for me.
script.ja: えっと、一番高い点とか、何かの最終的な結果、最高潮って感じです。今年の夏は、僕にとっては暑さとストレスのピークですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Yes, it's been a particularly hot summer.
script.ja: ええ、特に暑い夏だったわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Well, speaking of stress, I think that's our cue to wrap up for today.
script.ja: さて、ストレスの話といえば、今日はそろそろ終わりにする合図かなと思います。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Time flies when you're discussing the weather and geta.
script.ja: 天気と下駄について話していると時間が経つのが早いものね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Right. Thanks for joining us on The Daily Tanu-chan Show, everyone. Have a great day!
script.ja: ですね。ザ・デイリー・タヌちゃんショーにご参加いただき、ありがとうございました。皆さん、良い一日を！

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You too. Stay cool.
script.ja: あなたもね。涼しく過ごして。

news.title: Body found near hot spring resort believed to be 5-year-old boy who went missing in June
news.summary: Police in Kirishima City, Kagoshima Prefecture, said Friday that a body found in a river is likely that of a 5-year-old boy who went missing while bathing with…

news.title: Foreign visitors to Japan dip 2% in 1st half of 2026 as Chinese travelers drop
news.summary: The number of foreign visitors to Japan fell 2.0 percent from a year earlier to 21.1 million in the first six months of 2026, government data showed Wednesday,…

news.title: Tokyo music venue looking for a band to live on the premises and perform every day for a week
news.summary: If you and your crew are passionate about music, live near Japan’s capital, and are looking for an exciting new challenge, then let it be known that a…

wikipedia.title: 下駄の日
wikipedia.description: 全国木製はきもの業組合連合会が制定。下駄の寸法に7寸7分という数字が使われたことから「7月」、下駄の歯形の「二」から「22日」になった。
